entities.name: MAIZE
entities.description: The crop species being studied for drought tolerance.

entities.name: DROUGHT TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of a plant to withstand drought conditions.

entities.name: JOINT LINKAGE–LINKAGE DISEQUILIBRIUM MAPPING
entities.description: A powerful approach for detecting quantitative trait loci by combining linkage and LD analysis.

entities.name: PARALLEL MAPPING
entities.description: A mapping approach involving independent linkage and LD analysis.

entities.name: INTEGRATED MAPPING
entities.description: A mapping approach where datasets are analyzed in combination.

entities.name: SINGLE NUCLEOTIDE POLYMORPHISM
entities.description: Genetic markers used to identify genomic regions associated with traits.

entities.name: DROUGHT-RESPONSE CANDIDATE GENES
entities.description: Genes suspected to be involved in drought response used to develop SNP markers.

entities.name: RECOMBINANT INBRED LINE POPULATIONS
entities.description: Populations used for screening genetic markers.

entities.name: DIVERSE INBRED LINES
entities.description: Populations of diverse inbred lines used for screening genetic markers.

entities.name: ANTHESIS-SILKING INTERVAL
entities.description: An important trait used as a target for maize drought tolerance mapping.

entities.name: QUANTITATIVE TRAIT LOCI
entities.description: Regions of the genome associated with a quantitative trait.

entities.name: HAPLOTYPES
entities.description: Groups of alleles inherited together, used to improve mapping efficiency.

entities.name: PHENOTYPIC VARIATION EXPLAINED
entities.description: The proportion of phenotypic variance attributed to a specific genetic marker or locus.

entities.name: SET DOMAIN PROTEIN
entities.description: A protein involved in the control of flowering time.

entities.name: FLOWERING TIME
entities.description: The timing of the transition from vegetative to reproductive growth.

entities.name: ALDO/KETO REDUCTASE
entities.description: An enzyme associated with detoxification pathways.

entities.name: DETOXIFICATION PATHWAYS
entities.description: Biological processes that reduce the toxicity of harmful substances.

entities.name: ENVIRONMENTAL STRESS
entities.description: External factors that cause cellular damage and stress to the plant.
